contradictions: Manifest imports section claims D5 specified shadcn/ui ('Tech stack (Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui)'), but D5's technology stack table and decision ledger do not mention shadcn/ui anywhere. The shadcn/ui selection is actually a new decision made within Phase 6A itself (D55-P6A-003). The imports line should not attribute this selection to D5.

Appendix B Contrast Verification Table summary states '2 failing combinations identified and mitigated' but then enumerates 3 numbered items (Gold-500 on light, Warning-icon as small text, Gray-400 as placeholder). The table itself shows 3 combinations that fail the 4.5:1 normal text threshold. The count '2' is incorrect.

WCAG target is upgraded from 2.1 AA (specified in D2 Appendix D Section D.3) to 2.2 AA without acknowledging the deviation from the prior deliverable. While WCAG 2.2 is backward-compatible and a reasonable upgrade, the change should be explicitly noted as a divergence from D2's specification, following the precedent set by D3 which explicitly notes all divergences from D1.
warnings: Typography scale specifies line-height 1.5 for both Body and Body Small rows but assigns the Tailwind class 'leading-relaxed', which in standard Tailwind maps to line-height 1.625, not 1.5. If 1.5 is intended, the correct class is 'leading-normal'. This mismatch will produce inconsistent rendering if Phase 7 developers follow the numeric value vs. the Tailwind class.

The @theme CSS configuration block (Section 2.1.7) only defines color and font-family tokens. Spacing, border-radius, shadow, z-index, and transition tokens documented elsewhere in the deliverable are not included, meaning the theme block is an incomplete implementation reference. Phase 7 developers will need additional Tailwind configuration not shown here.

Z-index levels z-60 (Toast) and z-70 (Tooltip) are not part of Tailwind's default utility scale and are not defined in the @theme configuration. Custom utilities or explicit arbitrary values (e.g., z-[60]) would be needed, but no implementation guidance is provided for these custom levels.

Data visualization palette is described as 'tested against three forms of color blindness' in Section 2.1.6 but the same section immediately adds a 'verification requirement' stating the colors must be verified using Coblis before Phase 7 finalization. The testing status is ambiguous — either the testing was completed (in which case the verification note is redundant) or it was not (in which case the 'tested' claim is premature).

Feature references for the data visualization palette cite 'Growth/Expansion tier features F-060 through F-065', but per D3, F-060 (Course Profitability Dashboard) is Growth tier while F-061 through F-065 are all Expansion tier. The grouping is imprecise.
